paper_title: Implementing spatially explicit wind-driven seed and pollen dispersal in the individual-based larch simulation model: LAVESI-WIND 1.0

methods.method_name: Model Parameterisation to Fit Field Data
methods.description: The process of revising model parameters to achieve simulated tree densities comparable to field data, using forest inventory data and climate series.

steps.description: Forest inventory data were recorded for each larch individual with explicit positions on plots of a minimum area of 20 x 20 m for several locations along a density gradient.
steps.searchable_summary: record forest inventory data for larch individuals

steps.description: Simulations were conducted on 100 x 100 m areas with closed boundaries initialised by introducing 1,000 seeds in the first 100 years of a stabilisation period of 1,000 years.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct simulations with initial seed introduction

steps.description: Visually compared the number of trees at year 2011 from the central 20 x 20 m area to the field survey data.
steps.searchable_summary: compare simulation tree data to field survey data

steps.description: Parameters were manually tuned and iteratively performed simulation runs to improve the simulation results until achieving similar stand densities as observed.
steps.searchable_summary: tune model parameters to match observed stand densities

methods.description: Experiments conducted to evaluate the memory load and computation time of the model, including parallelisation and regression analysis.

steps.description: The memory load was estimated by adding up the size of all data types within each handled structure simulating a plot of one hectare.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate memory load for simulation structures

steps.description: Parallelised the code for estimating pollination probabilities, seed dispersal, and tree density computation using the OMP-library.
The performance of the model was evaluated by recording the computation time of each single simulation year for complete simulation runs.
steps.searchable_summary: record computation time for simulation runs

steps.description: Analysed the dependency between the time needed for each simulated year and the number of trees and seeds by generalised nonparametric regression.
steps.searchable_summary: perform regression analysis on computation time and tree/seeds count
